title: System Engineer (M365 Workplace)
description:
Summary of the Role :
The Microsoft 365 Workplace Engineer will focus on creating a seamless and productive work environment by deploying, troubleshooting and managing workplace technologies, ensuring optimal performance and user satisfaction. This role focuses on delivering effective support for cloud technologies and other tools, including but not limited to Azure, M365, and AWS components.

Responsibilities:

  • Respond to and resolve service desk tickets in a timely and efficient manner.
  • Continuously monitor alerts from endpoint management tool.
  • Provide second- and third-level technical support to end users for software or applications, hardware, and network issues.
  • Assist with the installation, configuration, and maintenance of IT equipment and software.
  • Set up and configure workstations for new employees, including installing necessary software and peripherals.
  • Perform regular maintenance and updates on systems/ workstations within the workplace scope to ensure optimal performance.
  • Work closely with other IT team members and departments to resolve complex technical issues.
  • Communicate effectively with end users to understand their needs and provide appropriate solutions.

Requirements:

  • Degree or Diploma in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in end-user computing or a similar role.
  • Familiar with cloud services and solutions (e.g., Microsoft 365. Office 365, Azure, AWS).
  • Familiar with Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) solutions like VMware Horizon, Microsoft Cloud PC, Workspace One or Citrix.
  • In-depth Knowledge of following :-

- SharePoint, Microsoft Purview, DLP, Microsoft Defender, AWS Connect.
- Automation tool such as Power Automate.
- Scripting languages such as PowerShell.

  • Strong knowledge of Windows and MacOS operating systems.
  • Experience with endpoint management solutions such as Microsoft SCCM, Intune, Ivanti or similar.
  • Experience with mobile device management (MDM) solutions.
  • Experience with IT Service Management frameworks like ITIL with ITIL V3/V4 Foundation.
  • Understanding of network fundamentals, including TCP/IP, DNS, and DHCP.
  • Required Certifications such as CompTIA A+, M365 Fundamentals, Microsoft Endpoint Administrator Associate.
  • Relevant Certifications such as Azure Administrator Associate, Microsoft SharePoint, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, AWS Certified AI Practitioner or similar are a plus.
  • Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
